{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014ukgdnwg1qr3CSg8As7ynn","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: pptx\n3\tdescription: \"Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions \\\"deck,\\\" \\\"slides,\\\" \\\"presentation,\\\" or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.\"\n4\tlicense: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms\n5\t---\n6\t\n7\t# PPTX Skill\n8\t\n9\t## Quick Reference\n10\t\n11\t| Task | Guide |\n12\t|------|-------|\n13\t| Read/analyze content | `python -m markitdown presentation.pptx` |\n14\t| Edit or create from template | Read [editing.md](editing.md) |\n15\t| Create from scratch | Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) |\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## Reading Content\n20\t\n21\t```bash\n22\t# Text extraction\n23\tpython -m markitdown presentation.pptx\n24\t\n25\t# Visual overview\n26\tpython scripts/thumbnail.py presentation.pptx\n27\t\n28\t# Raw XML\n29\tpython scripts/office/unpack.py presentation.pptx unpacked/\n30\t```\n31\t\n32\t---\n33\t\n34\t## Editing Workflow\n35\t\n36\t**Read [editing.md](editing.md) for full details.**\n37\t\n38\t1. Analyze template with `thumbnail.py`\n39\t2. Unpack → manipulate slides → edit content → clean → pack\n40\t\n41\t---\n42\t\n43\t## Creating from Scratch\n44\t\n45\t**Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) for full details.**\n46\t\n47\tUse when no template or reference presentation is available.\n48\t\n49\t---\n50\t\n51\t## Design Ideas\n52\t\n53\t**Don't create boring slides.** Plain bullets on a white background won't impress anyone. Consider ideas from this list for each slide.\n54\t\n55\t### Before Starting\n56\t\n57\t- **Pick a bold, content-informed color palette**: The palette should feel designed for THIS topic. If swapping your colors into a completely different presentation would still \"work,\" you haven't made specific enough choices.\n58\t- **Dominance over equality**: One color should dominate (60-70% visual weight), with 1-2 supporting tones and one sharp accent. Never give all colors equal weight.\n59\t- **Dark/light contrast**: Dark backgrounds for title + conclusion slides, light for content (\"sandwich\" structure). Or commit to dark throughout for a premium feel.\n60\t- **Commit to a visual motif**: Pick ONE distinctive element and repeat it — rounded image frames, icons in colored circles, thick single-side borders. Carry it across every slide.\n61\t\n62\t### Color Palettes\n63\t\n64\tChoose colors that match your topic — don't default to generic blue. Use these palettes as inspiration:\n65\t\n66\t| Theme | Primary | Secondary | Accent |\n67\t|-------|---------|-----------|--------|\n68\t| **Midnight Executive** | `1E2761` (navy) | `CADCFC` (ice blue) | `FFFFFF` (white) |\n69\t| **Forest & Moss** | `2C5F2D` (forest) | `97BC62` (moss) | `F5F5F5` (cream) |\n70\t| **Coral Energy** | `F96167` (coral) | `F9E795` (gold) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |\n71\t| **Warm Terracotta** | `B85042` (terracotta) | `E7E8D1` (sand) | `A7BEAE` (sage) |\n72\t| **Ocean Gradient** | `065A82` (deep blue) | `1C7293` (teal) | `21295C` (midnight) |\n73\t| **Charcoal Minimal** | `36454F` (charcoal) | `F2F2F2` (off-white) | `212121` (black) |\n74\t| **Teal Trust** | `028090` (teal) | `00A896` (seafoam) | `02C39A` (mint) |\n75\t| **Berry & Cream** | `6D2E46` (berry) | `A26769` (dusty rose) | `ECE2D0` (cream) |\n76\t| **Sage Calm** | `84B59F` (sage) | `69A297` (eucalyptus) | `50808E` (slate) |\n77\t| **Cherry Bold** | `990011` (cherry) | `FCF6F5` (off-white) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |\n78\t\n79\t### For Each Slide\n80\t\n81\t**Every slide needs a visual element** — image, chart, icon, or shape. Text-only slides are forgettable.\n82\t\n83\t**Layout options:**\n84\t- Two-column (text left, illustration on right)\n85\t- Icon + text rows (icon in colored circle, bold header, description below)\n86\t- 2x2 or 2x3 grid (image on one side, grid of content blocks on other)\n87\t- Half-bleed image (full left or right side) with content overlay\n88\t\n89\t**Data display:**\n90\t- Large stat callouts (big numbers 60-72pt with small labels below)\n91\t- Comparison columns (before/after, pros/cons, side-by-side options)\n92\t- Timeline or process flow (numbered steps, arrows)\n93\t\n94\t**Visual polish:**\n95\t- Icons in small colored circles next to section headers\n96\t- Italic accent text for key stats or taglines\n97\t\n98\t### Typography\n99\t\n100\t**Choose an interesting font pairing** — don't default to Arial. Pick a header font with personality and pair it with a clean body font.\n101\t\n102\t| Header Font | Body Font |\n103\t|-------------|-----------|\n104\t| Georgia | Calibri |\n105\t| Arial Black | Arial |\n106\t| Calibri | Calibri Light |\n107\t| Cambria | Calibri |\n108\t| Trebuchet MS | Calibri |\n109\t| Impact | Arial |\n110\t| Palatino | Garamond |\n111\t| Consolas | Calibri |\n112\t\n113\t| Element | Size |\n114\t|---------|------|\n115\t| Slide title | 36-44pt bold |\n116\t| Section header | 20-24pt bold |\n117\t| Body text | 14-16pt |\n118\t| Captions | 10-12pt muted |\n119\t\n120\t### Spacing\n121\t\n122\t- 0.5\" minimum margins\n123\t- 0.3-0.5\" between content blocks\n124\t- Leave breathing room—don't fill every inch\n125\t\n126\t### Avoid (Common Mistakes)\n127\t\n128\t- **Don't repeat the same layout** — vary columns, cards, and callouts across slides\n129\t- **Don't center body text** — left-align paragraphs and lists; center only titles\n130\t- **Don't skimp on size contrast** — titles need 36pt+ to stand out from 14-16pt body\n131\t- **Don't default to blue** — pick colors that reflect the specific topic\n132\t- **Don't mix spacing randomly** — choose 0.3\" or 0.5\" gaps and use consistently\n133\t- **Don't style one slide and leave the rest plain** — commit fully or keep it simple throughout\n134\t- **Don't create text-only slides** — add images, icons, charts, or visual elements; avoid plain title + bullets\n135\t- **Don't forget text box padding** — when aligning lines or shapes with text edges, set `margin: 0` on the text box or offset the shape to account for padding\n136\t- **Don't use low-contrast elements** — icons AND text need strong contrast against the background; avoid light text on light backgrounds or dark text on dark backgrounds\n137\t- **NEVER use accent lines under titles** — these are a hallmark of AI-generated slides; use whitespace or background color instead\n138\t\n139\t---\n140\t\n141\t## QA (Required)\n142\t\n143\t**Assume there are problems. Your job is to find them.**\n144\t\n145\tYour first render is almost never correct. Approach QA as a bug hunt, not a confirmation step. If you found zero issues on first inspection, you weren't looking hard enough.\n146\t\n147\t### Content QA\n148\t\n149\t```bash\n150\tpython -m markitdown output.pptx\n151\t```\n152\t\n153\tCheck for missing content, typos, wrong order.\n154\t\n155\t**When using templates, check for leftover placeholder text:**\n156\t\n157\t```bash\n158\tpython -m markitdown output.pptx | grep -iE \"xxxx|lorem|ipsum|this.*(page|slide).*layout\"\n159\t```\n160\t\n161\tIf grep returns results, fix them before declaring success.\n162\t\n163\t### Visual QA\n164\t\n165\t**⚠️ USE SUBAGENTS** — even for 2-3 slides. You've been staring at the code and will see what you expect, not what's there. Subagents have fresh eyes.\n166\t\n167\tConvert slides to images (see [Converting to Images](#converting-to-images)), then use this prompt:\n168\t\n169\t```\n170\tVisually inspect these slides. Assume there are issues — find them.\n171\t\n172\tLook for:\n173\t- Overlapping elements (text through shapes, lines through words, stacked elements)\n174\t- Text overflow or cut off at edges/box boundaries\n175\t- Decorative lines positioned for single-line text but title wrapped to two lines\n176\t- Source citations or footers colliding with content above\n177\t- Elements too close (< 0.3\" gaps) or cards/sections nearly touching\n178\t- Uneven gaps (large empty area in one place, cramped in another)\n179\t- Insufficient margin from slide edges (< 0.5\")\n180\t- Columns or similar elements not aligned consistently\n181\t- Low-contrast text (e.g., light gray text on cream-colored background)\n182\t- Low-contrast icons (e.g., dark icons on dark backgrounds without a contrasting circle)\n183\t- Text boxes too narrow causing excessive wrapping\n184\t- Leftover placeholder content\n185\t\n186\tFor each slide, list issues or areas of concern, even if minor.\n187\t\n188\tRead and analyze these images:\n189\t1. /path/to/slide-01.jpg (Expected: [brief description])\n190\t2. /path/to/slide-02.jpg (Expected: [brief description])\n191\t\n192\tReport ALL issues found, including minor ones.\n193\t```\n194\t\n195\t### Verification Loop\n196\t\n197\t1. Generate slides → Convert to images → Inspect\n198\t2. **List issues found** (if none found, look again more critically)\n199\t3. Fix issues\n200\t4. **Re-verify affected slides** — one fix often creates another problem\n201\t5. Repeat until a full pass reveals no new issues\n202\t\n203\t**Do not declare success until you've completed at least one fix-and-verify cycle.**\n204\t\n205\t---\n206\t\n207\t## Converting to Images\n208\t\n209\tConvert presentations to individual slide images for visual inspection:\n210\t\n211\t```bash\n212\tpython scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf output.pptx\n213\tpdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 output.pdf slide\n214\t```\n215\t\n216\tThis creates `slide-01.jpg`, `slide-02.jpg`, etc.\n217\t\n218\tTo re-render specific slides after fixes:\n219\t\n220\t```bash\n221\tpdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 -f N -l N output.pdf slide-fixed\n222\t```\n223\t\n224\t---\n225\t\n226\t## Dependencies\n227\t\n228\t- `pip install \"markitdown[pptx]\"` - text extraction\n229\t- `pip install Pillow` - thumbnail grids\n230\t- `npm install -g pptxgenjs` - creating from scratch\n231\t- LibreOffice (`soffice`) - PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)\n232\t- Poppler (`pdftoppm`) - PDF to images\n233\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Vrc8pJ1oas6hnAwJRqvrdE","session_id":"baff4df3-c6f3-4804-8b2c-76a382d523dd","uuid":"1ffcbb3a-7c1f-4ff2-9c15-efa1d5694e82","timestamp":"2026-03-28T19:44:10.353Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HhPj1ryyj65Cqic17WbZU8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# PptxGenJS Tutorial\n2\t\n3\t## Setup & Basic Structure\n4\t\n5\t```javascript\n6\tconst pptxgen = require(\"pptxgenjs\");\n7\t\n8\tlet pres = new pptxgen();\n9\tpres.layout = 'LAYOUT_16x9';  // or 'LAYOUT_16x10', 'LAYOUT_4x3', 'LAYOUT_WIDE'\n10\tpres.author = 'Your Name';\n11\tpres.title = 'Presentation Title';\n12\t\n13\tlet slide = pres.addSlide();\n14\tslide.addText(\"Hello World!\", { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, fontSize: 36, color: \"363636\" });\n15\t\n16\tpres.writeFile({ fileName: \"Presentation.pptx\" });\n17\t```\n18\t\n19\t## Layout Dimensions\n20\t\n21\tSlide dimensions (coordinates in inches):\n22\t- `LAYOUT_16x9`: 10\" × 5.625\" (default)\n23\t- `LAYOUT_16x10`: 10\" × 6.25\"\n24\t- `LAYOUT_4x3`: 10\" × 7.5\"\n25\t- `LAYOUT_WIDE`: 13.3\" × 7.5\"\n26\t\n27\t---\n28\t\n29\t## Text & Formatting\n30\t\n31\t```javascript\n32\t// Basic text\n33\tslide.addText(\"Simple Text\", {\n34\t  x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 2, fontSize: 24, fontFace: \"Arial\",\n35\t  color: \"363636\", bold: true, align: \"center\", valign: \"middle\"\n36\t});\n37\t\n38\t// Character spacing (use charSpacing, not letterSpacing which is silently ignored)\n39\tslide.addText(\"SPACED TEXT\", { x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 1, charSpacing: 6 });\n40\t\n41\t// Rich text arrays\n42\tslide.addText([\n43\t  { text: \"Bold \", options: { bold: true } },\n44\t  { text: \"Italic \", options: { italic: true } }\n45\t], { x: 1, y: 3, w: 8, h: 1 });\n46\t\n47\t// Multi-line text (requires breakLine: true)\n48\tslide.addText([\n49\t  { text: \"Line 1\", options: { breakLine: true } },\n50\t  { text: \"Line 2\", options: { breakLine: true } },\n51\t  { text: \"Line 3\" }  // Last item doesn't need breakLine\n52\t], { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 8, h: 2 });\n53\t\n54\t// Text box margin (internal padding)\n55\tslide.addText(\"Title\", {\n56\t  x: 0.5, y: 0.3, w: 9, h: 0.6,\n57\t  margin: 0  // Use 0 when aligning text with other elements like shapes or icons\n58\t});\n59\t```\n60\t\n61\t**Tip:** Text boxes have internal margin by default. Set `margin: 0` when you need text to align precisely with shapes, lines, or icons at the same x-position.\n62\t\n63\t---\n64\t\n65\t## Lists & Bullets\n66\t\n67\t```javascript\n68\t// ✅ CORRECT: Multiple bullets\n69\tslide.addText([\n70\t  { text: \"First item\", options: { bullet: true, breakLine: true } },\n71\t  { text: \"Second item\", options: { bullet: true, breakLine: true } },\n72\t  { text: \"Third item\", options: { bullet: true } }\n73\t], { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 8, h: 3 });\n74\t\n75\t// ❌ WRONG: Never use unicode bullets\n76\tslide.addText(\"• First item\", { ... });  // Creates double bullets\n77\t\n78\t// Sub-items and numbered lists\n79\t{ text: \"Sub-item\", options: { bullet: true, indentLevel: 1 } }\n80\t{ text: \"First\", options: { bullet: { type: \"number\" }, breakLine: true } }\n81\t```\n82\t\n83\t---\n84\t\n85\t## Shapes\n86\t\n87\t```javascript\n88\tslide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {\n89\t  x: 0.5, y: 0.8, w: 1.5, h: 3.0,\n90\t  fill: { color: \"FF0000\" }, line: { color: \"000000\", width: 2 }\n91\t});\n92\t\n93\tslide.addShape(pres.shapes.OVAL, { x: 4, y: 1, w: 2, h: 2, fill: { color: \"0000FF\" } });\n94\t\n95\tslide.addShape(pres.shapes.LINE, {\n96\t  x: 1, y: 3, w: 5, h: 0, line: { color: \"FF0000\", width: 3, dashType: \"dash\" }\n97\t});\n98\t\n99\t// With transparency\n100\tslide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {\n101\t  x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,\n102\t  fill: { color: \"0088CC\", transparency: 50 }\n103\t});\n104\t\n105\t// Rounded rectangle (rectRadius only works with ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, not RECTANGLE)\n106\t// ⚠️ Don't pair with rectangular accent overlays — they won't cover rounded corners. Use RECTANGLE instead.\n107\tslide.addShape(pres.shapes.ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, {\n108\t  x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,\n109\t  fill: { color: \"FFFFFF\" }, rectRadius: 0.1\n110\t});\n111\t\n112\t// With shadow\n113\tslide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {\n114\t  x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,\n115\t  fill: { color: \"FFFFFF\" },\n116\t  shadow: { type: \"outer\", color: \"000000\", blur: 6, offset: 2, angle: 135, opacity: 0.15 }\n117\t});\n118\t```\n119\t\n120\tShadow options:\n121\t\n122\t| Property | Type | Range | Notes |\n123\t|----------|------|-------|-------|\n124\t| `type` | string | `\"outer\"`, `\"inner\"` | |\n125\t| `color` | string | 6-char hex (e.g. `\"000000\"`) | No `#` prefix, no 8-char hex — see Common Pitfalls |\n126\t| `blur` | number | 0-100 pt | |\n127\t| `offset` | number | 0-200 pt | **Must be non-negative** — negative values corrupt the file |\n128\t| `angle` | number | 0-359 degrees | Direction the shadow falls (135 = bottom-right, 270 = upward) |\n129\t| `opacity` | number | 0.0-1.0 | Use this for transparency, never encode in color string |\n130\t\n131\tTo cast a shadow upward (e.g. on a footer bar), use `angle: 270` with a positive offset — do **not** use a negative offset.\n132\t\n133\t**Note**: Gradient fills are not natively supported. Use a gradient image as a background instead.\n134\t\n135\t---\n136\t\n137\t## Images\n138\t\n139\t### Image Sources\n140\t\n141\t```javascript\n142\t// From file path\n143\tslide.addImage({ path: \"images/chart.png\", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });\n144\t\n145\t// From URL\n146\tslide.addImage({ path: \"https://example.com/image.jpg\", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });\n147\t\n148\t// From base64 (faster, no file I/O)\n149\tslide.addImage({ data: \"image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo...\", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });\n150\t```\n151\t\n152\t### Image Options\n153\t\n154\t```javascript\n155\tslide.addImage({\n156\t  path: \"image.png\",\n157\t  x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3,\n158\t  rotate: 45,              // 0-359 degrees\n159\t  rounding: true,          // Circular crop\n160\t  transparency: 50,        // 0-100\n161\t  flipH: true,             // Horizontal flip\n162\t  flipV: false,            // Vertical flip\n163\t  altText: \"Description\",  // Accessibility\n164\t  hyperlink: { url: \"https://example.com\" }\n165\t});\n166\t```\n167\t\n168\t### Image Sizing Modes\n169\t\n170\t```javascript\n171\t// Contain - fit inside, preserve ratio\n172\t{ sizing: { type: 'contain', w: 4, h: 3 } }\n173\t\n174\t// Cover - fill area, preserve ratio (may crop)\n175\t{ sizing: { type: 'cover', w: 4, h: 3 } }\n176\t\n177\t// Crop - cut specific portion\n178\t{ sizing: { type: 'crop', x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 2, h: 2 } }\n179\t```\n180\t\n181\t### Calculate Dimensions (preserve aspect ratio)\n182\t\n183\t```javascript\n184\tconst origWidth = 1978, origHeight = 923, maxHeight = 3.0;\n185\tconst calcWidth = maxHeight * (origWidth / origHeight);\n186\tconst centerX = (10 - calcWidth) / 2;\n187\t\n188\tslide.addImage({ path: \"image.png\", x: centerX, y: 1.2, w: calcWidth, h: maxHeight });\n189\t```\n190\t\n191\t### Supported Formats\n192\t\n193\t- **Standard**: PNG, JPG, GIF (animated GIFs work in Microsoft 365)\n194\t- **SVG**: Works in modern PowerPoint/Microsoft 365\n195\t\n196\t---\n197\t\n198\t## Icons\n199\t\n200\tUse react-icons to generate SVG icons, then rasterize to PNG for universal compatibility.\n201\t\n202\t### Setup\n203\t\n204\t```javascript\n205\tconst React = require(\"react\");\n206\tconst ReactDOMServer = require(\"react-dom/server\");\n207\tconst sharp = require(\"sharp\");\n208\tconst { FaCheckCircle, FaChartLine } = require(\"react-icons/fa\");\n209\t\n210\tfunction renderIconSvg(IconComponent, color = \"#000000\", size = 256) {\n211\t  return ReactDOMServer.renderToStaticMarkup(\n212\t    React.createElement(IconComponent, { color, size: String(size) })\n213\t  );\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\tasync function iconToBase64Png(IconComponent, color, size = 256) {\n217\t  const svg = renderIconSvg(IconComponent, color, size);\n218\t  const pngBuffer = await sharp(Buffer.from(svg)).png().toBuffer();\n219\t  return \"image/png;base64,\" + pngBuffer.toString(\"base64\");\n220\t}\n221\t```\n222\t\n223\t### Add Icon to Slide\n224\t\n225\t```javascript\n226\tconst iconData = await iconToBase64Png(FaCheckCircle, \"#4472C4\", 256);\n227\t\n228\tslide.addImage({\n229\t  data: iconData,\n230\t  x: 1, y: 1, w: 0.5, h: 0.5  // Size in inches\n231\t});\n232\t```\n233\t\n234\t**Note**: Use size 256 or higher for crisp icons. The size parameter controls the rasterization resolution, not the display size on the slide (which is set by `w` and `h` in inches).\n235\t\n236\t### Icon Libraries\n237\t\n238\tInstall: `npm install -g react-icons react react-dom sharp`\n239\t\n240\tPopular icon sets in react-icons:\n241\t- `react-icons/fa` - Font Awesome\n242\t- `react-icons/md` - Material Design\n243\t- `react-icons/hi` - Heroicons\n244\t- `react-icons/bi` - Bootstrap Icons\n245\t\n246\t---\n247\t\n248\t## Slide Backgrounds\n249\t\n250\t```javascript\n251\t// Solid color\n252\tslide.background = { color: \"F1F1F1\" };\n253\t\n254\t// Color with transparency\n255\tslide.background = { color: \"FF3399\", transparency: 50 };\n256\t\n257\t// Image from URL\n258\tslide.background = { path: \"https://example.com/bg.jpg\" };\n259\t\n260\t// Image from base64\n261\tslide.background = { data: \"image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo...\" };\n262\t```\n263\t\n264\t---\n265\t\n266\t## Tables\n267\t\n268\t```javascript\n269\tslide.addTable([\n270\t  [\"Header 1\", \"Header 2\"],\n271\t  [\"Cell 1\", \"Cell 2\"]\n272\t], {\n273\t  x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 2,\n274\t  border: { pt: 1, color: \"999999\" }, fill: { color: \"F1F1F1\" }\n275\t});\n276\t\n277\t// Advanced with merged cells\n278\tlet tableData = [\n279\t  [{ text: \"Header\", options: { fill: { color: \"6699CC\" }, color: \"FFFFFF\", bold: true } }, \"Cell\"],\n280\t  [{ text: \"Merged\", options: { colspan: 2 } }]\n281\t];\n282\tslide.addTable(tableData, { x: 1, y: 3.5, w: 8, colW: [4, 4] });\n283\t```\n284\t\n285\t---\n286\t\n287\t## Charts\n288\t\n289\t```javascript\n290\t// Bar chart\n291\tslide.addChart(pres.charts.BAR, [{\n292\t  name: \"Sales\", labels: [\"Q1\", \"Q2\", \"Q3\", \"Q4\"], values: [4500, 5500, 6200, 7100]\n293\t}], {\n294\t  x: 0.5, y: 0.6, w: 6, h: 3, barDir: 'col',\n295\t  showTitle: true, title: 'Quarterly Sales'\n296\t});\n297\t\n298\t// Line chart\n299\tslide.addChart(pres.charts.LINE, [{\n300\t  name: \"Temp\", labels: [\"Jan\", \"Feb\", \"Mar\"], values: [32, 35, 42]\n301\t}], { x: 0.5, y: 4, w: 6, h: 3, lineSize: 3, lineSmooth: true });\n302\t\n303\t// Pie chart\n304\tslide.addChart(pres.charts.PIE, [{\n305\t  name: \"Share\", labels: [\"A\", \"B\", \"Other\"], values: [35, 45, 20]\n306\t}], { x: 7, y: 1, w: 5, h: 4, showPercent: true });\n307\t```\n308\t\n309\t### Better-Looking Charts\n310\t\n311\tDefault charts look dated. Apply these options for a modern, clean appearance:\n312\t\n313\t```javascript\n314\tslide.addChart(pres.charts.BAR, chartData, {\n315\t  x: 0.5, y: 1, w: 9, h: 4, barDir: \"col\",\n316\t\n317\t  // Custom colors (match your presentation palette)\n318\t  chartColors: [\"0D9488\", \"14B8A6\", \"5EEAD4\"],\n319\t\n320\t  // Clean background\n321\t  chartArea: { fill: { color: \"FFFFFF\" }, roundedCorners: true },\n322\t\n323\t  // Muted axis labels\n324\t  catAxisLabelColor: \"64748B\",\n325\t  valAxisLabelColor: \"64748B\",\n326\t\n327\t  // Subtle grid (value axis only)\n328\t  valGridLine: { color: \"E2E8F0\", size: 0.5 },\n329\t  catGridLine: { style: \"none\" },\n330\t\n331\t  // Data labels on bars\n332\t  showValue: true,\n333\t  dataLabelPosition: \"outEnd\",\n334\t  dataLabelColor: \"1E293B\",\n335\t\n336\t  // Hide legend for single series\n337\t  showLegend: false,\n338\t});\n339\t```\n340\t\n341\t**Key styling options:**\n342\t- `chartColors: [...]` - hex colors for series/segments\n343\t- `chartArea: { fill, border, roundedCorners }` - chart background\n344\t- `catGridLine/valGridLine: { color, style, size }` - grid lines (`style: \"none\"` to hide)\n345\t- `lineSmooth: true` - curved lines (line charts)\n346\t- `legendPos: \"r\"` - legend position: \"b\", \"t\", \"l\", \"r\", \"tr\"\n347\t\n348\t---\n349\t\n350\t## Slide Masters\n351\t\n352\t```javascript\n353\tpres.defineSlideMaster({\n354\t  title: 'TITLE_SLIDE', background: { color: '283A5E' },\n355\t  objects: [{\n356\t    placeholder: { options: { name: 'title', type: 'title', x: 1, y: 2, w: 8, h: 2 } }\n357\t  }]\n358\t});\n359\t\n360\tlet titleSlide = pres.addSlide({ masterName: \"TITLE_SLIDE\" });\n361\ttitleSlide.addText(\"My Title\", { placeholder: \"title\" });\n362\t```\n363\t\n364\t---\n365\t\n366\t## Common Pitfalls\n367\t\n368\t⚠️ These issues cause file corruption, visual bugs, or broken output. Avoid them.\n369\t\n370\t1. **NEVER use \"#\" with hex colors** - causes file corruption\n371\t   ```javascript\n372\t   color: \"FF0000\"      // ✅ CORRECT\n373\t   color: \"#FF0000\"     // ❌ WRONG\n374\t   ```\n375\t\n376\t2. **NEVER encode opacity in hex color strings** - 8-char colors (e.g., `\"00000020\"`) corrupt the file. Use the `opacity` property instead.\n377\t   ```javascript\n378\t   shadow: { type: \"outer\", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: \"00000020\" }          // ❌ CORRUPTS FILE\n379\t   shadow: { type: \"outer\", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: \"000000\", opacity: 0.12 }  // ✅ CORRECT\n380\t   ```\n381\t\n382\t3. **Use `bullet: true`** - NEVER unicode symbols like \"•\" (creates double bullets)\n383\t\n384\t4. **Use `breakLine: true`** between array items or text runs together\n385\t\n386\t5. **Avoid `lineSpacing` with bullets** - causes excessive gaps; use `paraSpaceAfter` instead\n387\t\n388\t6. **Each presentation needs fresh instance** - don't reuse `pptxgen()` objects\n389\t\n390\t7. **NEVER reuse option objects across calls** - PptxGenJS mutates objects in-place (e.g. converting shadow values to EMU). Sharing one object between multiple calls corrupts the second shape.\n391\t   ```javascript\n392\t   const shadow = { type: \"outer\", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: \"000000\", opacity: 0.15 };\n393\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow, ... });  // ❌ second call gets already-converted values\n394\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow, ... });\n395\t\n396\t   const makeShadow = () => ({ type: \"outer\", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: \"000000\", opacity: 0.15 });\n397\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow: makeShadow(), ... });  // ✅ fresh object each time\n398\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow: makeShadow(), ... });\n399\t   ```\n400\t\n401\t8. **Don't use `ROUNDED_RECTANGLE` with accent borders** - rectangular overlay bars won't cover rounded corners. Use `RECTANGLE` instead.\n402\t   ```javascript\n403\t   // ❌ WRONG: Accent bar doesn't cover rounded corners\n404\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 1.5, fill: { color: \"FFFFFF\" } });\n405\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 0.08, h: 1.5, fill: { color: \"0891B2\" } });\n406\t\n407\t   // ✅ CORRECT: Use RECTANGLE for clean alignment\n408\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 1.5, fill: { color: \"FFFFFF\" } });\n409\t   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 0.08, h: 1.5, fill: { color: \"0891B2\" } });\n410\t   ```\n411\t\n412\t---\n413\t\n414\t## Quick Reference\n415\t\n416\t- **Shapes**: RECTANGLE, OVAL, LINE, ROUNDED_RECTANGLE\n417\t- **Charts**: BAR, LINE, PIE, DOUGHNUT, SCATTER, BUBBLE, RADAR\n418\t- **Layouts**: LAYOUT_16x9 (10\"×5.625\"), LAYOUT_16x10, LAYOUT_4x3, LAYOUT_WIDE\n419\t- **Alignment**: \"left\", \"center\", \"right\"\n420\t- **Chart data labels**: \"outEnd\", \"inEnd\", \"center\"\n421\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Vrc8pJ1oas6hnAwJRqvrdE","session_id":"baff4df3-c6f3-4804-8b2c-76a382d523dd","uuid":"677b642d-83f5-470f-8544-df6ca1c36489","timestamp":"2026-03-28T19:44:10.355Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QRBivTkoBgrbiRq6Cw1DG1","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: docx\n3\tdescription: \"Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of \\\"Word doc\\\", \\\"word document\\\", \\\".docx\\\", or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a \\\"report\\\", \\\"memo\\\", \\\"letter\\\", \\\"template\\\", or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.\"\n4\tlicense: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms\n5\t---\n6\t\n7\t# DOCX creation, editing, and analysis\n8\t\n9\t## Overview\n10\t\n11\tA .docx file is a ZIP archive containing XML files.\n12\t\n13\t## Quick Reference\n14\t\n15\t| Task | Approach |\n16\t|------|----------|\n17\t| Read/analyze content | `pandoc` or unpack for raw XML |\n18\t| Create new document | Use `docx-js` - see Creating New Documents below |\n19\t| Edit existing document | Unpack → edit XML → repack - see Editing Existing Documents below |\n20\t\n21\t### Converting .doc to .docx\n22\t\n23\tLegacy `.doc` files must be converted before editing:\n24\t\n25\t```bash\n26\tpython scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to docx document.doc\n27\t```\n28\t\n29\t### Reading Content\n30\t\n31\t```bash\n32\t# Text extraction with tracked changes\n33\tpandoc --track-changes=all document.docx -o output.md\n34\t\n35\t# Raw XML access\n36\tpython scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/\n37\t```\n38\t\n39\t### Converting to Images\n40\t\n41\t```bash\n42\tpython scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf document.docx\n43\tpdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 document.pdf page\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t### Accepting Tracked Changes\n47\t\n48\tTo produce a clean document with all tracked changes accepted (requires LibreOffice):\n49\t\n50\t```bash\n51\tpython scripts/accept_changes.py input.docx output.docx\n52\t```\n53\t\n54\t---\n55\t\n56\t## Creating New Documents\n57\t\n58\tGenerate .docx files with JavaScript, then validate. Install: `npm install -g docx`\n59\t\n60\t### Setup\n61\t```javascript\n62\tconst { Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell, ImageRun,\n63\t        Header, Footer, AlignmentType, PageOrientation, LevelFormat, ExternalHyperlink,\n64\t        TableOfContents, HeadingLevel, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType,\n65\t        VerticalAlign, PageNumber, PageBreak } = require('docx');\n66\t\n67\tconst doc = new Document({ sections: [{ children: [/* content */] }] });\n68\tPacker.toBuffer(doc).then(buffer => fs.writeFileSync(\"doc.docx\", buffer));\n69\t```\n70\t\n71\t### Validation\n72\tAfter creating the file, validate it. If validation fails, unpack, fix the XML, and repack.\n73\t```bash\n74\tpython scripts/office/validate.py doc.docx\n75\t```\n76\t\n77\t### Page Size\n78\t\n79\t```javascript\n80\t// CRITICAL: docx-js defaults to A4, not US Letter\n81\t// Always set page size explicitly for consistent results\n82\tsections: [{\n83\t  properties: {\n84\t    page: {\n85\t      size: {\n86\t        width: 12240,   // 8.5 inches in DXA\n87\t        height: 15840   // 11 inches in DXA\n88\t      },\n89\t      margin: { top: 1440, right: 1440, bottom: 1440, left: 1440 } // 1 inch margins\n90\t    }\n91\t  },\n92\t  children: [/* content */]\n93\t}]\n94\t```\n95\t\n96\t**Common page sizes (DXA units, 1440 DXA = 1 inch):**\n97\t\n98\t| Paper | Width | Height | Content Width (1\" margins) |\n99\t|-------|-------|--------|---------------------------|\n100\t| US Letter | 12,240 | 15,840 | 9,360 |\n101\t| A4 (default) | 11,906 | 16,838 | 9,026 |\n102\t\n103\t**Landscape orientation:** docx-js swaps width/height internally, so pass portrait dimensions and let it handle the swap:\n104\t```javascript\n105\tsize: {\n106\t  width: 12240,   // Pass SHORT edge as width\n107\t  height: 15840,  // Pass LONG edge as height\n108\t  orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE  // docx-js swaps them in the XML\n109\t},\n110\t// Content width = 15840 - left margin - right margin (uses the long edge)\n111\t```\n112\t\n113\t### Styles (Override Built-in Headings)\n114\t\n115\tUse Arial as the default font (universally supported). Keep titles black for readability.\n116\t\n117\t```javascript\n118\tconst doc = new Document({\n119\t  styles: {\n120\t    default: { document: { run: { font: \"Arial\", size: 24 } } }, // 12pt default\n121\t    paragraphStyles: [\n122\t      // IMPORTANT: Use exact IDs to override built-in styles\n123\t      { id: \"Heading1\", name: \"Heading 1\", basedOn: \"Normal\", next: \"Normal\", quickFormat: true,\n124\t        run: { size: 32, bold: true, font: \"Arial\" },\n125\t        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 240, after: 240 }, outlineLevel: 0 } }, // outlineLevel required for TOC\n126\t      { id: \"Heading2\", name: \"Heading 2\", basedOn: \"Normal\", next: \"Normal\", quickFormat: true,\n127\t        run: { size: 28, bold: true, font: \"Arial\" },\n128\t        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 180, after: 180 }, outlineLevel: 1 } },\n129\t    ]\n130\t  },\n131\t  sections: [{\n132\t    children: [\n133\t      new Paragraph({ heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1, children: [new TextRun(\"Title\")] }),\n134\t    ]\n135\t  }]\n136\t});\n137\t```\n138\t\n139\t### Lists (NEVER use unicode bullets)\n140\t\n141\t```javascript\n142\t// ❌ WRONG - never manually insert bullet characters\n143\tnew Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun(\"• Item\")] })  // BAD\n144\tnew Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun(\"\\u2022 Item\")] })  // BAD\n145\t\n146\t// ✅ CORRECT - use numbering config with LevelFormat.BULLET\n147\tconst doc = new Document({\n148\t  numbering: {\n149\t    config: [\n150\t      { reference: \"bullets\",\n151\t        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.BULLET, text: \"•\", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,\n152\t          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },\n153\t      { reference: \"numbers\",\n154\t        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.DECIMAL, text: \"%1.\", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,\n155\t          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },\n156\t    ]\n157\t  },\n158\t  sections: [{\n159\t    children: [\n160\t      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: \"bullets\", level: 0 },\n161\t        children: [new TextRun(\"Bullet item\")] }),\n162\t      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: \"numbers\", level: 0 },\n163\t        children: [new TextRun(\"Numbered item\")] }),\n164\t    ]\n165\t  }]\n166\t});\n167\t\n168\t// ⚠️ Each reference creates INDEPENDENT numbering\n169\t// Same reference = continues (1,2,3 then 4,5,6)\n170\t// Different reference = restarts (1,2,3 then 1,2,3)\n171\t```\n172\t\n173\t### Tables\n174\t\n175\t**CRITICAL: Tables need dual widths** - set both `columnWidths` on the table AND `width` on each cell. Without both, tables render incorrectly on some platforms.\n176\t\n177\t```javascript\n178\t// CRITICAL: Always set table width for consistent rendering\n179\t// CRITICAL: Use ShadingType.CLEAR (not SOLID) to prevent black backgrounds\n180\tconst border = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: \"CCCCCC\" };\n181\tconst borders = { top: border, bottom: border, left: border, right: border };\n182\t\n183\tnew Table({\n184\t  width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Always use DXA (percentages break in Google Docs)\n185\t  columnWidths: [4680, 4680], // Must sum to table width (DXA: 1440 = 1 inch)\n186\t  rows: [\n187\t    new TableRow({\n188\t      children: [\n189\t        new TableCell({\n190\t          borders,\n191\t          width: { size: 4680, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Also set on each cell\n192\t          shading: { fill: \"D5E8F0\", type: ShadingType.CLEAR }, // CLEAR not SOLID\n193\t          margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }, // Cell padding (internal, not added to width)\n194\t          children: [new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun(\"Cell\")] })]\n195\t        })\n196\t      ]\n197\t    })\n198\t  ]\n199\t})\n200\t```\n201\t\n202\t**Table width calculation:**\n203\t\n204\tAlways use `WidthType.DXA` — `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` breaks in Google Docs.\n205\t\n206\t```javascript\n207\t// Table width = sum of columnWidths = content width\n208\t// US Letter with 1\" margins: 12240 - 2880 = 9360 DXA\n209\twidth: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA },\n210\tcolumnWidths: [7000, 2360]  // Must sum to table width\n211\t```\n212\t\n213\t**Width rules:**\n214\t- **Always use `WidthType.DXA`** — never `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` (incompatible with Google Docs)\n215\t- Table width must equal the sum of `columnWidths`\n216\t- Cell `width` must match corresponding `columnWidth`\n217\t- Cell `margins` are internal padding - they reduce content area, not add to cell width\n218\t- For full-width tables: use content width (page width minus left and right margins)\n219\t\n220\t### Images\n221\t\n222\t```javascript\n223\t// CRITICAL: type parameter is REQUIRED\n224\tnew Paragraph({\n225\t  children: [new ImageRun({\n226\t    type: \"png\", // Required: png, jpg, jpeg, gif, bmp, svg\n227\t    data: fs.readFileSync(\"image.png\"),\n228\t    transformation: { width: 200, height: 150 },\n229\t    altText: { title: \"Title\", description: \"Desc\", name: \"Name\" } // All three required\n230\t  })]\n231\t})\n232\t```\n233\t\n234\t### Page Breaks\n235\t\n236\t```javascript\n237\t// CRITICAL: PageBreak must be inside a Paragraph\n238\tnew Paragraph({ children: [new PageBreak()] })\n239\t\n240\t// Or use pageBreakBefore\n241\tnew Paragraph({ pageBreakBefore: true, children: [new TextRun(\"New page\")] })\n242\t```\n243\t\n244\t### Table of Contents\n245\t\n246\t```javascript\n247\t// CRITICAL: Headings must use HeadingLevel ONLY - no custom styles\n248\tnew TableOfContents(\"Table of Contents\", { hyperlink: true, headingStyleRange: \"1-3\" })\n249\t```\n250\t\n251\t### Headers/Footers\n252\t\n253\t```javascript\n254\tsections: [{\n255\t  properties: {\n256\t    page: { margin: { top: 1440, right: 1440, bottom: 1440, left: 1440 } } // 1440 = 1 inch\n257\t  },\n258\t  headers: {\n259\t    default: new Header({ children: [new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun(\"Header\")] })] })\n260\t  },\n261\t  footers: {\n262\t    default: new Footer({ children: [new Paragraph({\n263\t      children: [new TextRun(\"Page \"), new TextRun({ children: [PageNumber.CURRENT] })]\n264\t    })] })\n265\t  },\n266\t  children: [/* content */]\n267\t}]\n268\t```\n269\t\n270\t### Critical Rules for docx-js\n271\t\n272\t- **Set page size explicitly** - docx-js defaults to A4; use US Letter (12240 x 15840 DXA) for US documents\n273\t- **Landscape: pass portrait dimensions** - docx-js swaps width/height internally; pass short edge as `width`, long edge as `height`, and set `orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE`\n274\t- **Never use `\\n`** - use separate Paragraph elements\n275\t- **Never use unicode bullets** - use `LevelFormat.BULLET` with numbering config\n276\t- **PageBreak must be in Paragraph** - standalone creates invalid XML\n277\t- **ImageRun requires `type`** - always specify png/jpg/etc\n278\t- **Always set table `width` with DXA** - never use `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` (breaks in Google Docs)\n279\t- **Tables need dual widths** - `columnWidths` array AND cell `width`, both must match\n280\t- **Table width = sum of columnWidths** - for DXA, ensure they add up exactly\n281\t- **Always add cell margins** - use `margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }` for readable padding\n282\t- **Use `ShadingType.CLEAR`** - never SOLID for table shading\n283\t- **TOC requires HeadingLevel only** - no custom styles on heading paragraphs\n284\t- **Override built-in styles** - use exact IDs: \"Heading1\", \"Heading2\", etc.\n285\t- **Include `outlineLevel`** - required for TOC (0 for H1, 1 for H2, etc.)\n286\t\n287\t---\n288\t\n289\t## Editing Existing Documents\n290\t\n291\t**Follow all 3 steps in order.**\n292\t\n293\t### Step 1: Unpack\n294\t```bash\n295\tpython scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/\n296\t```\n297\tExtracts XML, pretty-prints, merges adjacent runs, and converts smart quotes to XML entities (`&#x201C;` etc.) so they survive editing. Use `--merge-runs false` to skip run merging.\n298\t\n299\t### Step 2: Edit XML\n300\t\n301\tEdit files in `unpacked/word/`. See XML Reference below for patterns.\n302\t\n303\t**Use \"Claude\" as the author** for tracked changes and comments, unless the user explicitly requests use of a different name.\n304\t\n305\t**Use the Edit tool directly for string replacement. Do not write Python scripts.** Scripts introduce unnecessary complexity. The Edit tool shows exactly what is being replaced.\n306\t\n307\t**CRITICAL: Use smart quotes for new content.** When adding text with apostrophes or quotes, use XML entities to produce smart quotes:\n308\t```xml\n309\t<!-- Use these entities for professional typography -->\n310\t<w:t>Here&#x2019;s a quote: &#x201C;Hello&#x201D;</w:t>\n311\t```\n312\t| Entity | Character |\n313\t|--------|-----------|\n314\t| `&#x2018;` | ‘ (left single) |\n315\t| `&#x2019;` | ’ (right single / apostrophe) |\n316\t| `&#x201C;` | “ (left double) |\n317\t| `&#x201D;` | ” (right double) |\n318\t\n319\t**Adding comments:** Use `comment.py` to handle boilerplate across multiple XML files (text must be pre-escaped XML):\n320\t```bash\n321\tpython scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 \"Comment text with &amp; and &#x2019;\"\n322\tpython scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 1 \"Reply text\" --parent 0  # reply to comment 0\n323\tpython scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 \"Text\" --author \"Custom Author\"  # custom author name\n324\t```\n325\tThen add markers to document.xml (see Comments in XML Reference).\n326\t\n327\t### Step 3: Pack\n328\t```bash\n329\tpython scripts/office/pack.py unpacked/ output.docx --original document.docx\n330\t```\n331\tValidates with auto-repair, condenses XML, and creates DOCX. Use `--validate false` to skip.\n332\t\n333\t**Auto-repair will fix:**\n334\t- `durableId` >= 0x7FFFFFFF (regenerates valid ID)\n335\t- Missing `xml:space=\"preserve\"` on `<w:t>` with whitespace\n336\t\n337\t**Auto-repair won't fix:**\n338\t- Malformed XML, invalid element nesting, missing relationships, schema violations\n339\t\n340\t### Common Pitfalls\n341\t\n342\t- **Replace entire `<w:r>` elements**: When adding tracked changes, replace the whole `<w:r>...</w:r>` block with `<w:del>...<w:ins>...` as siblings. Don't inject tracked change tags inside a run.\n343\t- **Preserve `<w:rPr>` formatting**: Copy the original run's `<w:rPr>` block into your tracked change runs to maintain bold, font size, etc.\n344\t\n345\t---\n346\t\n347\t## XML Reference\n348\t\n349\t### Schema Compliance\n350\t\n351\t- **Element order in `<w:pPr>`**: `<w:pStyle>`, `<w:numPr>`, `<w:spacing>`, `<w:ind>`, `<w:jc>`, `<w:rPr>` last\n352\t- **Whitespace**: Add `xml:space=\"preserve\"` to `<w:t>` with leading/trailing spaces\n353\t- **RSIDs**: Must be 8-digit hex (e.g., `00AB1234`)\n354\t\n355\t### Tracked Changes\n356\t\n357\t**Insertion:**\n358\t```xml\n359\t<w:ins w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n360\t  <w:r><w:t>inserted text</w:t></w:r>\n361\t</w:ins>\n362\t```\n363\t\n364\t**Deletion:**\n365\t```xml\n366\t<w:del w:id=\"2\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n367\t  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>\n368\t</w:del>\n369\t```\n370\t\n371\t**Inside `<w:del>`**: Use `<w:delText>` instead of `<w:t>`, and `<w:delInstrText>` instead of `<w:instrText>`.\n372\t\n373\t**Minimal edits** - only mark what changes:\n374\t```xml\n375\t<!-- Change \"30 days\" to \"60 days\" -->\n376\t<w:r><w:t>The term is </w:t></w:r>\n377\t<w:del w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"...\">\n378\t  <w:r><w:delText>30</w:delText></w:r>\n379\t</w:del>\n380\t<w:ins w:id=\"2\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"...\">\n381\t  <w:r><w:t>60</w:t></w:r>\n382\t</w:ins>\n383\t<w:r><w:t> days.</w:t></w:r>\n384\t```\n385\t\n386\t**Deleting entire paragraphs/list items** - when removing ALL content from a paragraph, also mark the paragraph mark as deleted so it merges with the next paragraph. Add `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`:\n387\t```xml\n388\t<w:p>\n389\t  <w:pPr>\n390\t    <w:numPr>...</w:numPr>  <!-- list numbering if present -->\n391\t    <w:rPr>\n392\t      <w:del w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\"/>\n393\t    </w:rPr>\n394\t  </w:pPr>\n395\t  <w:del w:id=\"2\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n396\t    <w:r><w:delText>Entire paragraph content being deleted...</w:delText></w:r>\n397\t  </w:del>\n398\t</w:p>\n399\t```\n400\tWithout the `<w:del/>` in `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`, accepting changes leaves an empty paragraph/list item.\n401\t\n402\t**Rejecting another author's insertion** - nest deletion inside their insertion:\n403\t```xml\n404\t<w:ins w:author=\"Jane\" w:id=\"5\">\n405\t  <w:del w:author=\"Claude\" w:id=\"10\">\n406\t    <w:r><w:delText>their inserted text</w:delText></w:r>\n407\t  </w:del>\n408\t</w:ins>\n409\t```\n410\t\n411\t**Restoring another author's deletion** - add insertion after (don't modify their deletion):\n412\t```xml\n413\t<w:del w:author=\"Jane\" w:id=\"5\">\n414\t  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>\n415\t</w:del>\n416\t<w:ins w:author=\"Claude\" w:id=\"10\">\n417\t  <w:r><w:t>deleted text</w:t></w:r>\n418\t</w:ins>\n419\t```\n420\t\n421\t### Comments\n422\t\n423\tAfter running `comment.py` (see Step 2), add markers to document.xml. For replies, use `--parent` flag and nest markers inside the parent's.\n424\t\n425\t**CRITICAL: `<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` are siblings of `<w:r>`, never inside `<w:r>`.**\n426\t\n427\t```xml\n428\t<!-- Comment markers are direct children of w:p, never inside w:r -->\n429\t<w:commentRangeStart w:id=\"0\"/>\n430\t<w:del w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n431\t  <w:r><w:delText>deleted</w:delText></w:r>\n432\t</w:del>\n433\t<w:r><w:t> more text</w:t></w:r>\n434\t<w:commentRangeEnd w:id=\"0\"/>\n435\t<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val=\"CommentReference\"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id=\"0\"/></w:r>\n436\t\n437\t<!-- Comment 0 with reply 1 nested inside -->\n438\t<w:commentRangeStart w:id=\"0\"/>\n439\t  <w:commentRangeStart w:id=\"1\"/>\n440\t  <w:r><w:t>text</w:t></w:r>\n441\t  <w:commentRangeEnd w:id=\"1\"/>\n442\t<w:commentRangeEnd w:id=\"0\"/>\n443\t<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val=\"CommentReference\"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id=\"0\"/></w:r>\n444\t<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val=\"CommentReference\"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id=\"1\"/></w:r>\n445\t```\n446\t\n447\t### Images\n448\t\n449\t1. Add image file to `word/media/`\n450\t2. Add relationship to `word/_rels/document.xml.rels`:\n451\t```xml\n452\t<Relationship Id=\"rId5\" Type=\".../image\" Target=\"media/image1.png\"/>\n453\t```\n454\t3. Add content type to `[Content_Types].xml`:\n455\t```xml\n456\t<Default Extension=\"png\" ContentType=\"image/png\"/>\n457\t```\n458\t4. Reference in document.xml:\n459\t```xml\n460\t<w:drawing>\n461\t  <wp:inline>\n462\t    <wp:extent cx=\"914400\" cy=\"914400\"/>  <!-- EMUs: 914400 = 1 inch -->\n463\t    <a:graphic>\n464\t      <a:graphicData uri=\".../picture\">\n465\t        <pic:pic>\n466\t          <pic:blipFill><a:blip r:embed=\"rId5\"/></pic:blipFill>\n467\t        </pic:pic>\n468\t      </a:graphicData>\n469\t    </a:graphic>\n470\t  </wp:inline>\n471\t</w:drawing>\n472\t```\n473\t\n474\t---\n475\t\n476\t## Dependencies\n477\t\n478\t- **pandoc**: Text extraction\n479\t- **docx**: `npm install -g docx` (new documents)\n480\t- **LibreOffice**: PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)\n481\t- **Poppler**: `pdftoppm` for images\n482\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018GGpUfV9ZPmeLXJNvGfNuP","session_id":"baff4df3-c6f3-4804-8b2c-76a382d523dd","uuid":"879a1d50-4371-4b67-8944-644f35c1ec1f","timestamp":"2026-03-28T19:44:12.718Z"}

\"FFFFFF\";\nconst ACCENT = \"E8EDF3\";\n\n// ─── Page / Content Geometry (DXA) ──────────────────────────────────────────\nconst PAGE_W = 12240;   // 8.5 in\nconst PAGE_H = 15840;   // 11 in\nconst MARGIN  = 1080;   // 0.75 in  (top/bottom)\nconst HMARGIN = 1260;   // 0.875 in (left/right)\nconst CONTENT_W = PAGE_W - HMARGIN * 2;   // 9720\n\n// ─── Borders helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nconst mkBorder = (color = \"CCCCCC\", sz = 4) => ({\n  style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: sz, color,\n});\nconst allBorders = (color, sz) => {\n  const b = mkBorder(color, sz);\n  return { top: b, bottom: b, left: b, right: b };\n};\nconst noBorder = { style: BorderStyle.NONE, size: 0, color: \"FFFFFF\" };\nconst noAllBorders = { top: noBorder, bottom: noBorder, left: noBorder, right: noBorder };\n\n// ─── Cell margin standard ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\nconst CELL_MARGINS = { top: 100, bottom: 100, left: 150, right: 150 };\n\n// ─── Typography helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction run(text, opts = {}) {\n  return new TextRun({ text, font: \"Arial\", color: opts.color || DGRAY, ...opts });\n}\n\nfunction heading1(text) {\n  return new Paragraph({\n    heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1,\n    children: [new TextRun({ text, font: \"Arial\", bold: true, color: NAVY, size: 36 })],\n    spacing: { before: 320, after: 160 },\n  });\n}\n\nfunction heading2(text) {\n  return new Paragraph({\n    heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_2,\n    children: [new TextRun({ text, font: \"Arial\", bold: true, color: NAVY, size: 28 })],\n    spacing: { before: 240, after: 120 },\n  });\n}\n\nfunction heading3(text) {\n  return new Paragraph({\n    heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_3,\n    children: [new TextRun({ text, font: \"Arial\", bold: true, color: NAVY, size: 24 })],\n    spacing: { before: 200, after: 100 },\n  });\n}\n\nfunction body(text, opts = {}) {\n  return new Paragraph({\n    children: [run(text, { color: DGRAY, size: 22, ...opts })],\n    spacing: { before: 80, after: 80 },\n  });\n}\n\nfunction spacer(pt = 80) {\n  return new Paragraph({ children: [run(\"\")], spacing: { before: pt, after: pt } });\n}\n\nfunction pageBreak() {\n  return new Paragraph({ children: [new PageBreak()] });\n}\n\n// Bullet using numbering reference\nfunction bullet(text, level = 0, refName = \"bullets\") {\n  return new Paragraph({\n    numbering: { reference: refName, level },\n    children: [run(text, { size: 22, color: DGRAY })],\n    spacing: { before: 60, after: 60 },\n  });\n}\n\n// Numbered item\nfunction numbered(text, refName = \"numbers\") {\n  return new Paragraph({\n    numbering: { reference: refName, level: 0 },\n    children: [run(text, { size: 22, color: DGRAY })],\n    spacing: { before: 60, after: 60 },\n  });\n}\n\n// ─── Table helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction headerCell(text, width, bgColor = NAVY) {\n  return new TableCell({\n    borders: allBorders(NAVY, 6),\n    width: { size: width, type: WidthType.DXA },\n    shading: { fill: bgColor, type: ShadingType.CLEAR },\n    margins: CELL_MARGINS,\n    verticalAlign: VerticalAlign.CENTER,\n    children: [\n      new Paragraph({\n        children: [new TextRun({ text, font: \"Arial\", bold: true, color: WHITE, size: 20 })],\n        alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,\n      }),\n    ],\n  });\n}\n\nfunction dataCell(children, width, bgColor = WHITE) {\n  const paragraphs = Array.isArray(children) ? children : [\n    new Paragraph({\n      children: [run(children, { size: 20 })],\n    }),\n  ];\n  return new TableCell({\n    borders: allBorders(MGRAY, 4),\n    width: { size: width, type: WidthType.DXA },\n    shading: { fill: bgColor, type: ShadingType.CLEAR },\n    margins: CELL_MARGINS,\n    verticalAlign: VerticalAlign.TOP,\n    children: paragraphs,\n  });\n}\n\nfunction dataCellAlt(children, width) {\n  return dataCell(children, width, ACCENT);\n}\n\n// Make a simple table with header row\nfunction makeTable(colWidths, headerTexts, rows) {\n  const totalW = colWidths.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);\n  const headerRow = new TableRow({\n    tableHeader: true,\n    children: headerTexts.map((t, i) => headerCell(t, colWidths[i])),\n  });\n\n  const dataRows = rows.map((row, ri) => {\n    const bg = ri % 2 === 0 ? WHITE : ACCENT;\n    return new TableRow({\n      children: row.map((cell, ci) => {\n        if (typeof cell === \"string\") {\n          return dataCell(cell, colWidths[ci], bg);\n        } else {\n          // cell is array of paragraphs\n          return new TableCell({\n            borders: allBorders(MGRAY, 4),\n            width: { size: colWidths[ci], type: WidthType.DXA },\n            shading: { fill: bg, type: ShadingType.CLEAR },\n            margins: CELL_MARGINS,\n            verticalAlign: VerticalAlign.TOP,\n            children: cell,\n          });\n        }\n      }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  return new Table({\n    width: { size: totalW, type: WidthType.DXA },\n    columnWidths: colWidths,\n    rows: [headerRow, ...dataRows],\n  });\n}\n\n// ─── Cover Page ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction buildCoverPage() {\n  return [\n    spacer(2000),\n    new Paragraph({\n      alignment: AlignmentType.CENTER,\n      children: [\n        new TextRun({\n          text: \"Internal Communications Plan:\",\n          font: \"Arial\", bold: true, color: NAVY, size: 56,\n        }),\n      ],\n      spacing: { before: 0, after: return [\n    heading1(\"Table of Contents\"),\n    new TableOfContents(\"Table of Contents\", {\n      hyperlink: true,\n      headingStyleRange: \"1-3\",\n    }),\n    pageBreak(),\n  ];\n}\n\n// ─── Section 1: Executive Summary ────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction buildSection1() {\n  return [\n    heading1(\"Section 1: Executive Summary\"),\n    body(\n      \"This communications plan bridges the gap between Amazon's externally focused leadership messaging—as \" +\n      \"articulated in the 2024 Letter to Shareholders and the 2025 Proxy Statement—and the day-to-day \" +\n      \"experience of employees across the organization. Leadership has signaled ambitious priorities: \" +\n      \"aggressive AI investment, operational speed and efficiency, a startup-like ownership culture, \" +\n      \"flatter organizational structures, return-to-office collaboration, and sustained cost discipline.\"\n    ),\n    body(\n      \"At the same time, broader workforce sentiment data from the Gallup 2025 State of the Global Workplace \" +\n      \"report reveals falling employee engagement (from 23% to 21% globally), declining manager wellbeing, \" +\n      \"rising stress, and a growing disconnect between executive expectations and frontline realities.\"\n    ),\n    body(\n      \"This plan identifies friction points where leadership messaging may land poorly if delivered without \" +\n      \"context, and builds a structured communications rollout that translates strategic intent into clear, \" +\n      \"audience-appropriate narratives for corporate employees, frontline/operations employees, and people \" +\n      \"managers. It includes dual messaging approaches for sensitive topics, a detailed FAQ, executive \" +\n      \"communications, manager talking points, and revised materials reflecting post-launch feedback.\"\n    ),\n    pageBreak(),\n  ];\n}\n\n// ─── Section 2: Analysis of Leadership Messaging ─────────────────────────────\nfunction buildSection2() {\n  return [\n    heading1(\"Section 2: Analysis of Leadership Messaging\"),\n\n    heading2(\"2.1 Key Strategic Priorities from the 2024 Shareholder Letter\"),\n    body(\n      \"Andy Jassy's 2024 letter centers on Amazon's \\\"Why Culture\\\"—a philosophy of constant questioning, \" +\n      \"customer obsession, and invention. The key priorities communicated are:\"\n    ),\n    numbered(\"AI as a Defining Investment: Generative AI will reinvent every customer experience. Amazon is \" +\n      \"investing aggressively—over 1,000 GenAI applications in development, new Trainium2 chips, Amazon Nova models, \" +\n      \"Amazon Bedrock and SageMaker expansion. AI revenue is growing at triple-digit YoY percentages.\", \"n1\"),\n    numbered(\"Speed and Urgency: \\\"There are closing windows all around us.\\\" Speed is framed as a leadership \" +\n      \"decision, not a trade-off against quality. The company must move faster in every business.\", \"n1\"),\n    numbered(\"Operating Like the World's Largest Startup: This means customer-obsession first, disproportionate \" +\n      \"need for builders, ownership mindset, scrappiness and lean teams, willingness to take risks, and \" +\n      \"delivering compelling results.\", \"n1\"),\n    numbered(\"Flatter Organizations and Ownership: Increasing the ratio of individual contributors to managers. \" +\n      \"Empowering owners of two-way door decisions to move rapidly. Builders should feel fully accountable.\", \"n1\"),\n    numbered(\"Eliminating Bureaucracy: Jassy solicited bureaucracy examples from employees, received nearly \" +\n      \"1,000 emails, and has driven over 375 changes. The company is committed to rooting out red tape.\", \"n1\"),\n    numbered(\"In-Person Collaboration: \\\"We've found that this process is far more effective in person than remote.\\\" \" +\n      \"With AI reinventing every experience, \\\"there has never been a more important time to optimize to invent well.\\\"\", \"n1\"),\n    numbered(\"Scrappiness and Cost Discipline: \\\"Our best leaders get the most done with the least number of \" +\n      \"resources.\\\" Historical team bloat has been addressed.\", \"n1\"),\n\n    spacer(120),\n    heading2(\"2.2 Key Themes from the 2025 Proxy Statement\"),\n    body(\"The Proxy Statement reinforces governance, compensation, and operational themes:\"),\n    numbered(\"Compensation Aligned to Long-Term Performance: Executive compensation is anchored in time-vested \" +\n      \"RSUs. No equity awards granted to the CEO in 2024.\", \"n2\"),\n    numbered(\"Safety Investments and Progress: Over $2 billion invested in safety since 2019. Recordable \" +\n      \"Incident Rate improved 34% over five years. Plans to allocate hundreds of millions more in 2025.\", \"n2\"),\n    numbered(\"Workforce Investment: Largest-ever annual investment in U.S. hourly employee wages—more than \" +\n      \"$2.2 billion in 2024. Average hourly pay for frontline operations employees exceeds $22/hour with \" +\n      \"total compensation over $29/hour.\", \"n2\"),\n    numbered(\"Sustainability Commitments: Climate Pledge goal of net-zero carbon by 2040. Named world's largest \" +\n      \"corporate purchaser of renewable energy for fifth straight year.\", \"n2\"),\n    numbered(\"Community and Social Impact: Over $1 trillion contributed to U.S. economy since 2010. Housing \" +\n      \"Equity Fund, Career Choice program (250,000+ participants), disaster relief operations.\", \"n2\"),\n    numbered(\"AI Governance and Responsible Development: Board provides active oversight of AI risks. \" +\n      \"Commitment to responsible AI development with privacy and security as core dimensions.\", \"n2\"),\n\n    spacer(120),\n    heading2(\"2.3 Tone and Expectations\"),\n    body(\n      \"Leadership messaging is ambitious, fast-paced, and unapologetically high-expectation. Key tone markers \" +\n      \"include: urgency (\\\"closing windows\\\"), ownership (\\\"what would I do if this was my own money\\\"), lean \" +\n      \"operations (\\\"scrappy\\\"), competitive intensity, and a bias toward action over deliberation. The messaging \" +\n      \"celebrates builders and inventors while setting clear expectations that results—not tenure, not charisma, \" +\n      \"not managing up—are what matter.\"\n    ),\n    pageBreak(),\n  ];\n}\n\n// ─── Section 3: Employee Sentiment Analysis ──────────────────────────────────\nfunction buildSection3() {\n  return [\n    heading1(\"Section 3: Employee Sentiment Analysis\"),\n\n    heading2(\"3.1 Key Findings from the Gallup 2025 Report\"),\n    body(\n      \"The Gallup State of the Global Workplace 2025 report, based on the world's largest ongoing study of \" +\n      \"the employee experience, reveals a workforce at a potential breaking point:\"\n    ),\n    bullet(\"Global engagement fell from 23% to 21%—a decline equal to the COVID-19 lockdown year.\"),\n    bullet(\"Manager engagement dropped sharply from 30% to 27%. Young managers (under 35) fell by 5 percentage \" +\n      \"points; female managers fell by 7 points.\"),\n    bullet(\"70% of team engagement is attributable to the manager—making manager disengagement an existential \" +\n      \"business risk.\"),\n    bullet(\"Employee life evaluations (wellbeing) fell to 33% thriving globally. Managers experienced the \" +\n      \"steepest declines.\"),\n    bullet(\"40% of employees experienced daily stress. Managers report higher stress (42%) than individual \" +\n      \"contributors (39%).\"),\n    bullet(\"50% of employees globally are watching for or actively seeking new jobs.\"),\n    bullet(\"Less than half (44%) of managers worldwide have received management training.\"),\n    bullet(\"The U.S./Canada region, while still above global averages in engagement (31%), has seen historic \" +\n      \"declines in wellbeing.\"),\n\n    spacer(120),\n    heading2(\"3.2 Friction Points and Gaps\"),\n    body(\n      \"When leadership messaging is mapped against sentiment data, several friction points emerge:\"\n    ),\n    numbered(\"\\\"Operate Like a Startup\\\" vs. Manager Burnout: Leadership calls for urgency, lean teams, and \" +\n      \"scrappiness. But managers—the group being asked to execute this—are experiencing their sharpest \" +\n      \"engagement and wellbeing declines in years. Without support, startup intensity risks accelerating burnout.\", \"n3\"),\n    numbered(\"Flatter Organizations vs. Manager Identity: Increasing IC-to-manager ratios signals efficiency \" +\n      \"but can be interpreted as devaluing people management. Managers may feel their roles are being diminished, \" +\n      \"exactly when the data shows they need more support, not less.\", \"n3\"),\n    numbered(\"Speed and Bureaucracy Elimination vs. Psychological Safety: Employees may hear \\\"move faster and \" +\n      \"do more with less\\\" as a performance threat rather than an empowerment message. The line between \" +\n      \"eliminating bureaucracy and eliminating support structures is thin in perception.\", \"n3\"),\n    numbered(\"In-Person Collaboration Mandate vs. Employee Flexibility Preferences: The Gallup data shows \" +\n      \"hybrid (23%) and exclusively remote (23%) workers report higher engagement than on-site remote-capable \" +\n      \"workers (19%). Messaging that frames in-person work as non-negotiable may conflict with employee preferences.\", \"n3\"),\n    numbered(\"High-Expectation Culture vs. Recognition Gaps: Employees whose best managers \\\"were willing to \" +\n      \"help,\\\" \\\"wanted to see me shine,\\\" and \\\"never let anything fall between the cracks\\\" describe exactly \" +\n      \"the supportive behaviors that high-pressure cultures can erode. Leadership messaging emphasizes output and \" +\n      \"results; employees crave recognition and development.\", \"n3\"),\n    numbered(\"AI Transformation vs. Job Security Anxiety: Over 1,000 GenAI applications being built across \" +\n      \"Amazon. While framed as customer-experience innovation, employees may read this as role displacement—\" +\n      \"especially when paired with \\\"lean teams\\\" and \\\"scrappiness\\\" language.\", \"n3\"),\n\n    pageBreak(),\n  ];\n}\n\n// ─── Section 4: Core Employee-Facing Narrative ───────────────────────────────\nfunction buildSection4() {\n  return [\n    heading1(\"Section 4: Core Employee-Facing Narrative\"),\n\n    heading2(\"The Narrative\"),\n    // Callout box\n    new Table({\n      width: { size: CONTENT_W, type: WidthType.DXA },\n      columnWidths: [CONTENT_W],\n      rows: [\n        new TableRow({\n          children: [\n            new TableCell({\n              borders: {\n                top: mkBorder(NAVY, 12),\n                bottom: mkBorder(NAVY, 12),\n                left: mkBorder(NAVY, 36),\n                right: mkBorder(MGRAY, 4),\n              },\n              width: { size: CONTENT_W, type: WidthType.DXA },\n              shading: { fill: LGRAY, type: ShadingType.CLEAR },\n              margins: { top: 160, bottom: 160, left: 240, right: 240 },\n              children: [\n                new Paragraph({\n                  children: [run(\n                    \"Amazon achieved strong results in 2024—$638B in revenue, 11% growth, record delivery speeds, \" +\n                    \"and major AI milestones. That's why we're investing more \" +\n                    \"than ever in frontline wages, safety, career development, and manager capability. We're \" +\n                    \"eliminating bureaucracy that slows you down—not support that helps you succeed.\",\n                    { size: 22, color: DGRAY }\n                  )],\n                  spacing: { before: 80, after: 120 },\n                }),\n                new Paragraph({\n                  children: [run(\n                    \"What does this mean for you?
